channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Conservative group slaps top federal agency with lawsuit over Biden-era handling of illegal immigrants
item.description: Center to Advance Security in America is suing the Department of Homeland Security for data related to the release of criminal illegal immigrants into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE's access to critical IRS system containing taxpayer information is imminent
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency, led by Elon Musk, will soon have access to an IRS system that contains sensitive taxpayer information, Fox News has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin sending foreign minister to Saudi Arabia for talks with Trump officials
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet with his Russian counterpart in Saudi Arabia on Tuesday to discuss a possible peace deal for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine and Europe worry about being sidelined as Trump pushes direct talks with Russia on war's end
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s approach to ending Russia’s war against Ukraine has left European allies and Ukrainian officials worried they are being largely sidelined by the new U.S. administration as Washington and Moscow plan direct negotiations.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor slams Vance's call for Europe to be inclusive of far-right parties
item.description: Olaf Scholz rebuked U.S. Vice President JD Vance for suggesting that far-right parties should not be shut out of government. Germany's other political parties have refused to work with the far-right AfD.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim and Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event. It's part of an ongoing terminology dispute over the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: The latest: Federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: Federal workers all over the country have responded with anger and confusion Friday toward President Donald Trump’s aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Musk's latest moves to shrink the federal workforce explained
item.description: President Trump and right-hand man Elon Musk have rolled out a series of moves to cull the federal workforce, forging ahead with numerous methods to fire employees that have already sparked legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Sarah Palin ‘would love to’ join Trump administration
item.description: She pointed to "gatekeepers" within the administration, telling NewsNation: "It gets frustrating for those with a heart to serve.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: PIX on Politics Panel breaks down Adams-DOJ controversy
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It’s been a politically charged week for Mayor Eric Adams after the Department of Justice ordered prosecutors to drop a federal corruption case against him.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: A framed image of President Donald Trump's mug shot from when he was indicted in Georgia in 2023 is hanging outside the Oval Office in the latest bit of redecorating since he moved back in.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ellison tells Minnesota schools to follow law if immigration authorities show up
item.description: Responding to the Trump administration’s decision to allow immigration enforcement in schools, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Friday advised district leaders to ensure that they and any federal officials who arrive at schools follow legal protocols.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: University of St. Thomas scrambling after Dept. of Ed cuts millions in funding
item.description: The $6.3 million grant was targeted under President Donald Trump’s effort to cut programs the government considers “diversity, equity and inclusion,” or DEI.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, and Lukas Levin, MPR News

item.title: Staff at CDC and NIH are reeling as Trump administration cuts workforce
item.description: As many as 1,300 probationary employees at CDC and 1,500 at NIH are losing their jobs. Many fear for the future of public health and scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, Pien Huang, and Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Calls for Mayor Adams’ resignation mount amid turmoil at DOJ
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Calls for Mayor Eric Adams’ resignation are mounting after the Department of Justice ordered his case to be dropped. One call came from Lt. Gov. Antonio Delgado, second-in-command to Gov. Kathy Hochul. Hochul is the only person with the power to remove Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: A federal worker tried to take Trump's 'Fork' resignation offer. Here's what happened
item.description: Liz Goggin, a social worker with the Veterans Health Administration, took the offer to resign in exchange for pay and benefits through September. Then she learned her position was exempt.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: What Sen. Tina Smith’s retirement means for Minnesota politics in 2026
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith announced she wouldn’t seek reelection on Thursday, opening a big field on both sides to replace her in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Brian Bakst, Alanna Elder, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Egg prices unite Nevada lawmakers as AB171 makes its way toward approval
item.description: Nevada lawmakers continued Tuesday to fast-track legislation that could bring down the price of eggs, pushing Assembly Bill 171 (AB171) closer to law.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: No, your student loans won't be forgiven if DOGE illegally accessed your data
item.description: No, you won't get your student loans forgiven if DOGE violated FERPA, even though TikTok videos claim otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Egg price bill advances at Nevada Legislature; AB171 could increase supply in 30 days
item.description: A bill aimed at reducing the skyrocketing price of eggs in Nevada advanced on Monday, but the legislative process will take some time even if there's no opposition.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Medical field needs more training like cultural competency, not less, Nevada lawmakers argue
item.description: A proposal to encourage — not require — medical practitioners to get training in subjects ranging from suicide prevention to cultural competency drew an extremely cold reaction from lawmakers on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
